The temple is richly decorated, its most distinctive feature being the 216 serenely smiling stone faces on the towers  ...    The similarity of the faces has encouraged many scholars to posit that they are representations of Jayavarman VII. Some others believe that the faces bear a likeness to Lokesvara. The two conclusions may be linked, as George Coedès, 10th century French scholar of southeast Asian archaeology and history, theorizes. Coedès says that Jayavarman considered himself as a 'devaraja' or god-king, in continuation with the beliefs of Khmer kings before him. The difference was that while his predecessors
